purpose: Models the feedback loop between stock prices and reported profitability through stock-based compensation
components: Rising stock prices → lower perceived SBC cost per option/RSU
Lower SBC cost → better reported non-GAAP margins
'SBC vega': sensitivity of reported margins to stock price
Creates GAAP vs. non-GAAP P/E gap (10-15 turns for pure-play AI, 1.5-2.0 turns for S&P 500)
current_assessment: The reflexive loop is currently amplifying apparent profitability. Aggregate Mag-7 SBC ~$75-80B annually. The mechanism works in reverse during drawdowns.
limitations: Quantifying the exact magnitude of the distortion requires knowing the true cost of SBC
The reflexive loop may persist indefinitely if markets remain in expansion
GAAP accounting partially captures SBC cost but market focuses on non-GAAP
Does not predict when the loop reverses
